text: The video displays a patient with a pantaloon hernia on the right side and a direct hernia on the left. The laparoscopic procedure involves bilateral inguinal hernia repair using a single large mesh placed in the preperitoneal space.

The surgeon encounters tight and hard adhesions while dissecting between the hernia sac and fascia transversalis. After reducing all hernias, the suprapubic window is opened to allow for the passage of a large mesh.
A tailored macro porous partially absorbable mesh, measuring fifteen by thirty centimeters, is shaped like a pond and positioned in the surgical field to prevent migration.
The surgeon fixes the large mesh bilaterally with absorbable tacks to Cooper's ligament. Subsequently, the peritoneal flap is closed using an absorbable suture.
